rationale: Delta b27e793b..75c17fd9 is exactly the requested minimal fix and nothing else: buildGateResolvedDoc backfills a nil substitutes map to map[string]string{} via a LOCAL variable (cfg not mutated, command stays read-only), plus a raw-JSON-string test assertion — correctly checked on the marshaled bytes, not unmarshal-into-struct, which would mask a null regression. Empirically re-verified with the rebuilt binary: zero-config `--gate bead --json` now emits "substitutes":{}; jq '.substitution.substitutes|keys' succeeds; jq 'paths(. == null)' over the whole doc is EMPTY — no member emits null in the default config; in_force correctly stays "claude_sub_on_quota" (computed off the original map's length; nil and {} agree). Re-ran the round-1 hostile-config probe: text escaping byte-identical, populated substitutes still round-trips byte-exact, all five gates emit exactly the 5 pinned members. Tests green (cmd/mindspec, internal/complete, internal/config). Round-1 F1: ADDRESSED.

findings.summary: Round-1 F1 (substitutes:null vs {} in the pinned R9 --gate --json contract) is closed by 75c17fd9: nil map normalized to {} in buildGateResolvedDoc (cmd/mindspec/config.go:457-466 region), guarded by a raw-bytes test asserting presence of '"substitutes":{}' AND absence of '"substitutes":null' on the nil-map path (config_test.go ~:555-565). Empirically reproduced the fix: default config now emits {"substitutes":{},"claude_sub_on_quota":true,"in_force":"claude_sub_on_quota"} and jq keys-indexing works. No new defect introduced: local-variable backfill (no cfg mutation, read-only holds), in_force flip semantics unchanged, text path unchanged (renderSubstitutes already handled empty), 5-member contract intact across all five gates, and a full-document null scan (jq paths(. == null)) is empty.

The delta touches only the substitutes nil-guard and its test — no render/escape function changed. Re-drove the round-1 hostile config (ANSI ESC, BEL, newline-forged recovery lines in note/model/lens/substitutes-key/substitutes-value) through the rebuilt binary: text output byte-identical to round 1 (all strconv.Quote'd, no raw control byte), and the populated hostile substitutes entry still round-trips byte-exact through encoding/json (jq decodes the key back to a raw newline). The round-1 escaping clearance stands.
